programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings fight for the last jar of Yummy with magic Mayan bracelets.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When his beloved plant is sold by mistake, Duck must lose his job at the garden centre in order to retrieve it from a top-security greenhouse.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog creates chaos in a loo roll factory, leaving Duck covered in toilet paper. Duck just wants to fix the machine, but Frog is convinced he’s a spooky mummy!

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: Max and Gark become famous singers after getting stuck together with bin juice.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum is a Little Bro in control! He’s pitting his Big Bro Adam against best mate Joe to see who will win a series of funny, gungy and gross challenges and who will land an awful forfeit from the Wheel of Doom in this epic competition. Callum's the Little Bro, but he’s in control, so Adam and Joe had better impress him! When Joe and Adam press play on a mysterious voice note, they know it’ll be a long but extra 24 hours. Little Bro Callum is challenging them to let him control their day. With tasks like bathing in cold mud, snacking on edible insects and wearing their pants on their head, nothing's normal in this high-key contest to be the best. After Callum’s decided on a winner, it’s onto the next funny challenge: will they choose to save their crab friend or their stuff? And why is a giant shark distracting them?!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro goes undercover for a special Children in Need prank on Apollo and recruits Sabre, Alfie Watts and Pudsey to help.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and the Witch's Ghost
programme.desc: Scooby and the gang take a detour to a haunted New England town.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When a witch struggles to master her new wand, Twinkle decides to have a go, only to make her friends vanish. She must now go on a solo quest to find them.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes find treasure as they bid to save Merhaven from an angry seaweed. Blossom is delighted with her bath bomb treasure, but rather than use it, she insists on bringing it along on the watery quest to save it for a special occasion. But as the angry seaweed nears Merhaven, Blossom realises she must finally sacrifice her treasure to save the day!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse’s light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The family is going on a canoe trip, and Boy and Girl are each allowed to bring a friend. For Girl, the choice is easy - she invites her BFF. But Boy realizes he doesn’t really have any friends outside his own family. The rest of the siblings set out to help Boy find a friend for the canoe trip - but can he overcome his shyness?

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: A last-second change of plans threatens The Next Step's chances of qualifying for regionals, and Ozzy struggles to step up to the challenge. When Richelle encounters a difficult hurdle, Lola feels pressure to stay loyal to her friend. Piper is surprised by some new feelings for an old friend.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Jim is organising History Week, and everyone has a role in the historical re-enactment. Jessica is Queen Elizabeth, while Aoife plays Mary Queen of Scots - but who will win the war?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Join Joel, Abby and Shini on an epic Northern Ireland adventure! Joel finds out how potatoes are turned into crisps and invents his very own brand new flavour! Shini learns how to care for horses before saddling up for a ride along the stunning coastline, and we find out who is crowned the ultimate Celtic warrior.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum is a Little Bro in control! He’s pitting his Big Bro Adam against best mate Joe to see who will win a series of funny, gungy and gross challenges and who will land an awful forfeit from the Wheel of Doom in this epic competition. Callum's the Little Bro, but he’s in control, so Adam and Joe had better impress him! When Joe and Adam press play on a mysterious voice note, they know it’ll be a long but extra 24 hours. Little Bro Callum is challenging them to let him control their day. With tasks like bathing in cold mud, snacking on edible insects and wearing their pants on their head, nothing's normal in this high-key contest to be the best. After Callum’s decided on a winner, it’s onto the next funny challenge: will they choose to save their crab friend or their stuff? And why is a giant shark distracting them?!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Orli and Ozzie must find two grown-ups who get lost in HQ’s most dangerous areas.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad go undercover at a villain wedding to stop the guests from creating mass oddness.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Lola and Richelle's friendship is threatened when Lola tries to keep Richelle from making the biggest mistake of her life. Noah's role as dance captain puts him at odds with Jacquie and puts pressure on their relationship. Ozzy is terrified he has ruined the team's shot at regionals.

programme.title: The Lady Grace Mysteries
programme.desc: When the Crown of St Benedict is stolen from a locked and guarded room in the court of Queen Elizabeth I, Lady Grace sets out to solve the mystery!

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: When Orbit’s plan moves too slowly, Tilda takes charge and teams up with an unlikely ally. But shapeshifting aliens and hidden traps make her mission a lot more complicated.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes searching for Patagonia’s perfect predator: the puma. After waiting his whole life to see one, Steve sees a tiny cub and witnesses a female puma hunting an armadillo.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Leanne is in the Ouch and About pod, and can Dr Xand use his voice to break glass? Dr Dan and Dr Raph work their abs with Gladiator Dynamite.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: The kids invent an admirer for Chef, hoping to make him happy enough to order them pizza.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: Duncan and Courtney make a quick escape to try and replace Chef's special energy drink.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Stuff is going missing all over the world, but the team are distracted, fascinated by Squawk’s new Futuroid camera, which incredibly spits out snaps of future events. DM is aghast when the camera produces a photo of him and Squawk kissing! When Penfold is abducted by an alien tractor-beam, Squawk and Danger Mouse must overcome their mutual disgust to work together. Their search for Penfold leads them to Flarg, the alien who’s been nabbing the world’s missing items. Chained upside down, and with Flarg intent on stealing everything in existence, the situation looks hopeless.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Animated series. Danger Mouse explodes back into action and is instantly dismissed for destroying half of London. Can the world survive without Danger Mouse?

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Cheese and Dog are teasing each other about the things they like to do. However, trying out each other's interests causes them to realise what they’ve been missing out on.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Mouse brings home an old ice cream truck, the kids start their own smoothie business. However, the town's lovable ice cream man, Uncle Bub, isn't happy about the competition.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When the family visits a farm, Cheese bonds with Delilah the cow over all things dairy. However, bringing her home to live with the family leads to all sorts of problems!

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings attempt to take back a chocolate egg from a group of penguins.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings transform the ice field into an open-air discotheque!

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings come up with a ride worthy of a county fair in the living room.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Hoofius has decided not to quest. Not because he would rather be doing a puzzle or playing his panpipes (though that is rather enjoyable), but because this will be his 1,000th quest and will be added to the Scroll of Honour for all to see for eternity. His friends reluctantly quest without him, but when they don’t return, Hoofius soon comes to regret staying behind.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Trevor is extremely proud of being the clever one in the gang and thinks that his smart mouth is his superpower. However, his friends feel that sometimes his jokes go too far - and they are right to be concerned! When Mr Friendlyface sends the heroes on a quest to prevent Toothgrinder the Ogre from messing up the village green, it’s Trevor’s smart mouth that puts all the heroes in danger.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are questing to find out who is behind a string of cake thefts, and go into detective mode. After questioning the baker, they decide to stage a stakeout at the bakery. However, when they spy cakes apparently walking off by themselves, they realise that there is more to this mystery than meets the eye - and a quest to find some missing cakes soon turns into a quest to save the entire forest!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog must move a famous pop star’s possessions from one mansion to another. However, they are unable to do it with anything remaining in one piece.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum on his own. To keep him company, Duck makes a Frankenstein Frog, which soon runs rampage. It’s up to Frog to save the day!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog work on a farm, Frog makes the perfect scarecrow. However, he gets covered in super-strong fertiliser and becomes a giant, monster-sized, chaos-causing Frog!

programme.title: The Adventures of Mansour: Age of AI
programme.desc: Mansour's 'Pop-Bot' advertising invention gets hacked by Blink and Tarek.

programme.title: The Adventures of Mansour: Age of AI
programme.desc: Mansour creates a simple bot named Carla to distract Blink.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog tries to bring a touch of class to his new job in a cafe, but Duck's desire to wash each plate to polished perfection is holding him up.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog work on a farm, Frog makes the perfect scarecrow. However, he gets covered in super-strong fertiliser and becomes a giant, monster-sized, chaos-causing Frog!

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s tiny Brussels Sprout performing I’m Mighty and proving how great he is.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: 'If you get shy then your friends can help you.' Bing and his friends put on a show in the park. After Coco's dance, Pando's song and Sula's acrobatics display, everyone is excited to see Bing's finale. When he gets up on the stage, Bing is overcome with stage fright, so his friends and rainbow fairy mice come to the rescue.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy and Laa-Laa drive to the Hidey Hup in the Tubby Car. They meet Tinky Winky and Po and the Teletubbies go on the Tubby Uppy Downy together.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Preschool animation. Tee is super excited when new flat-pack shelving arrives for his room. However, he soon discovers it is going to take some time for Mo to build it.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea plans to freeze Supertato into an ice cube by pretending to teach him to dance.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. Sula tells a frustrated Bing that you have to be loud when you do a show. But Bing is a little too loud, and when Coco joins the show, Bing's loud Biteysaurus isn't welcome, as he is hurting Sula’s ears!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston left an egg sandwich out for too long, and now its explosive smell is stinking up the whole town. Mojo and Bo need to evacuate everyone in Swoppiton before the eggy 'stinkplosion'!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Take a cooking class with Chippo the Cheetah, figure out why different animals are the same, and help the Squirrels stick together and search for Duggee.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie skips into the Clubhouse and greets her friends. Together, they reflect on their first meeting. Arriving with her parents in a long limousine, Norrie, her parents and her 72 siblings flood out of the car. It's just Norrie who is going to stay at the Clubhouse, though, and enjoy some well-needed quiet. She can play alone and hear herself think! But after a while, she misses the hustle and bustle, so Duggee introduces Norrie to Enid the cat. Together, they venture into the Clubhouse, and Norrie has a friend so she isn't alone, but she still has a little bit more room than she would have at home!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey’s bedtime story is disturbed by Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey must find a way to play postman and the ground's lava without squabbling with Bingo.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: When Nine needs a helping hand, he turns into a talented trio of circus blocks, the Three Threes. Learn how to share the number 9 into equal parts with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It's snowing! The Alphablocks whip up a wondrous winter wonderland, but can they get Mr Snowman to the North Pole before he melts? This episode has fun with 'an' and the words man, fun, sun, fan, tan, van and men.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Norman wants to get a pet dinosaur, but Nick and the Popcasters don't think this is a good idea. They try to convince Norman to pick another pet.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: It’s a hot day in Playtime Towers, so Bella helps Lift cool down with her flappy paper fan. This inspires Sketch to make flop-and-fold flutterby butterflies in Boxtropolis. In the Ice Disco, Hettie has a paper fan that’s perfect for the flap dance. Patch has a paper fan too, and it reminds him of the Wowee Peacocks' beautiful tail feathers, the perfect inspiration for his Fruity Fantails!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing finds a frog in the garden and wants to keep it, so he and Sula make the frog a 'house' in a bucket. But Froggy doesn't like her new home and hops out. Maybe they've not thought enough about what Froggy really needs in her home.

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘cucumber’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see cucumbers that are crunchy, mushy in the middle and curly. Dee meets a cool cucumber, and we meet a very cute baby one. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Declan causes a stir when he decides not to say 'please'.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Dana wants to cheer Wilf up and asks the Piggies to find something that will make him happy. But Wilf takes advantage of their kindness and requests a honking vehicle - just to annoy everyone! When it breaks, Wilf is even more miserable.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a kitchen, where they watch a man turn on a tap and fill a glass with water. When he leaves, the Twirlywoos come out and turn the tap on. They fill a glass with water and then start to find other things around the kitchen to fill up, progressing to sillier and sillier objects - ending with a wellington boot! Just as the sink fills up and begins to overflow, the Hooter calls the Twirlywoos home, and the man comes back to find his kitchen full of odd containers filled with water. Back in the Big Red Boat, it's time for tea. The fruit on top of the tea machine is a giant watermelon, which is so full of tea that the Twirlywoos have to go back for second and third helpings. They are so full that they have to lie down, leaving the coast clear for Peekaboo to come and drink up all the leftover tea.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends bake some delicious pancakes, whilst Mr Tumble and Baker Tumble make some of their own to feed to the other hungry Tumbles!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po drive the Tubby Car to the Hidey Hup and take turns on the Tubby Bouncy. Tinky Winky and Dipsy arrive and they have a go at bouncing too.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Remi, George, Zadie, Idris, Felicity, Tommy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyone’s bags in this episode are cardboard boxes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate cardboard boxes inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song ‘The Wheels on the Bus!’ features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Plus, the Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a building site with her new friend Dreya to play a game of I-spy.

programme.title: What’s in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Gardening expert Sue introduces the Fact Pack to roses. They discover that there are hundreds of types, why roses can smell lovely, and how their smell and flavour is in many different things.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It’s a special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphi has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphi to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: The gang rescue a trapped baby Watermelonsuchus with the help of some Lycheemodus.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While away camping, Bluey makes a new friend called Jean-Luc. Despite speaking different languages, they come up with a great game. Bluey is sad when Jean-Luc goes home unexpectedly but learns that even if friendships are fleeting they can still mean a lot.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasn't loud at all but impossibly quiet! As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in. But Roly is still too shy, so Duggee and Norrie start doing things with him, knowing this will help him to embrace the fun of the Clubhouse. While painting, playing with balloons and yelling, Roly begins to truly find his voice and be himself! Soon, Roly hardly noticed if his dad was around at all.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver returns with a song all about seasons. It’s a celebration of spring, summer, autumn and winter.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to the park to feed the birds at the pond. On the way, they race like racing cars, spot the different coloured flowers, listen to the thunder and even meet a Stick Man tree!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sisters Kyra and Khloe go on a big family day out to a farm. There are lots of exciting things to do, including a pony ride, or should that be a unicorn ride? They explore an amazing maze where the girls find a giant sunflower! But will they ever find their way back out? They also discover a very muddy and squelchy pumpkin patch and pick their perfect pumpkins! This Bafta-winning pre-school observational documentary follows the lives of young children and their families from a variety of backgrounds across the UK.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself. Dill pickle is a popular Polish ingredient and Kay gets his friends to taste it before they eat the soup. Not all of his friends like the taste at first, but Kay hopes they will all enjoy the finished soup. As well as dill pickle, he uses spring onions, potatoes and carrot in his soup. He then serves the delicious, hot soup in baked bread rolls with whipping cream on top.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: She goes on a training rescue mission with a lifeboat team to find out how the equipment on a lifeboat works. She shows us how the special thermal-imaging cameras are used to find people who are in trouble - even at night! Next, she visits a quarry to see how gravel, sand, cement and water are mixed together to make concrete. Maddie uses her special cameras to follow the journey the ingredients take from the quarry to the plant where everything is mixed together. We see how the mixing lorry then takes the concrete to the building site and how it is poured to make a road!

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks is a disability assistance dog for her owner Charli, who has a condition that makes it painful for her to pick things up or to push and pull things. Tinks uses her super fetch skills to help Charli every day. Charli is helping park ranger Pauline with some litter picking in Woofton Park. First they have to travel across the tricky terrain in the park to meet Pauline, so she can give them the instructions and equipment they need. Tinks persuades Charli to use the motor on Charli’s wheelchair at turbo speed! Tinks helps Charli find the litter, but the gate to the recycling bins is locked, and the recycling van is on its way. Can Tinks fetch park ranger Pauline in time to recycle their rubbish?

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some of the zoo’s acrobats, a family of fossa who are incredible climbers and enjoy jumping high up in the trees. Next, they go to see the leaf-tailed gecko, which has an amazing tail that looks just like a leaf to help camouflage it in the wild. Steve and Aneeshwar then reveal the surprising wild connection between them.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is unwell. He's in his pyjamas and under strict orders to stay in bed and rest. But poor Waffle can't get comfy. When he sees Evie and Simon playing in the garden, he feels left out and begins to woof and woof. Simon tells Waffle to rest quietly, but it's too late. The wonder dog's incessant barking has already disturbed Mrs Hobbs next door and, to make matters worse, like Waffle she's not feeling well either. Mrs Hobbs has a bad cold, and she's blaming it on the Brooklyn-Bells. She's convinced getting drenched in their ball-pit pond is the cause. The Brooklyn-Bells do chores for Mrs Hobbs to help her get better, and Jess brings home a surprise from work.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba help the seagulls defeat the Great Gull.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: It's the day the geese fly over on their annual migration south. When Sonny reports that geese put on an amazing display if you mimic their calls, the gang look for the right sound to get a response from the geese before they fly past!

programme.title: Maddie + Triggs
programme.desc: Mum is taking Maddie and Triggs to McGinty’s farm to hear his world-famous animal orchestra. But McGinty’s star performer, Gary the Chicken, won’t come out of his coop! Can Maddie use her musical skills and expert diplomacy to save the day?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: After breaking the Chickens' TV aerial during the finale of their favourite soap opera, the Squirrels take it upon themselves to recreate the final episode for them. But will their performance impress the grumpy Chickens?

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks are on an adventure exploring Fraction Island. They are curious to learn why there are four islands when their map only shows one. When they meet a new friend, One Whole, they realise that they need to explore all the islands and find more Fraction Friends if they want to solve the mystery!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock H has built a henhouse and needs a HEN to live in it. She and her friends end up making three hens and decide to give them names. The pen shows them capital letters, and Alphablock C explains that they need them for names. They name their hens PEN, JEN and ZEN. The hens love their name badges. Then, C introduces a parade of animals with different names, each one wearing a badge - CAM the ram, PAT the cat and ZAK the yak. There’s just one more name to be given - BEN the pen!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends learn all about getting their eyes tested. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble with some new glasses when he loses his old pair.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, then forwards again!

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how grasses play such an important role in the environment. They find out that grasses can be food for animals and some can be turned into ingredients to make food for people too. On the way, they discover how long grass can help shelter small animals and insects, and that cut grass can be useful too, before finding a lovely grassy place to relax and have fun!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running. Her aim is to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies' very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move because of propellers! Grace flies a seaplane, shows off an unusual hydrocopter and goes for a fast ride in a raceboat!

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: An unexpected visitor has arrived in the playground - a cat! The pups are initially scared. After all, they’ve heard that cats have lasers for eyes. But Dodge decides to be brave and talk to it. Dodge discovers the cat is friendly and isn’t anything like how the pups had imagined, she’s just lost. The pups decide to help her find her way to Kitten School.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is finding it hard to think of something new to do on Do Something New Day, so he heads to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny’s house to see if she has any ideas.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Kwazii and Tracker visit the desert for an Octo-Agent test but end up getting lost.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter and his friends mistake the echoey 'rebbits' of a lost Jeremy Fisher for a scary monster, they set about ridding their tunnels of this mysterious invader. But with Tommy Brock on the warpath nearby, it won't be easy!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels try the ultimate achievement of counting to 100 to earn their counting badge.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzag’s ADHD gives her a case of the hops, so Vida teaches her how to make a to-do list.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: It's movie night, and Supertato is thrilled to discover that the star is Prickly Pear - his special skill is rounding up Mini Peas with his pea-yodelling. But the film gives Evil Pea the idea to take over Tato Tower with Mini Peas, trapping Supertato inside. When Supertato is accidentally ejected out of Tato Tower, he must convince Prickly Pear to take up pea-yodelling again so they can both wrangle the Mini Peas back to the freezer.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings are throwing a party in the attic. Weasy thinks it’s a great idea until he realises that grown-ups are not invited! Feeling left out, he decides to throw his own, with chaotic results!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo are great at picking apples with Mojo’s crane top. But Preston’s drone might be even better... until it accidentally scoops up Felix. Mojo and Bo are on the chase!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey’s bedtime story is disturbed by Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Exhausted with requests for help, Looph deliberately disappears. The Piris turn detective, searching the berg with little success until her ingenious plan is finally revealed.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey must find a way to play postman and the ground's lava without squabbling with Bingo.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum jumps to conclusions when he hears about an upcoming volcanic eruption. But little does he know, it's Ava’s science project that’s set to fizzle in a safe experiment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Preschool comedy drama series. Jess hopes the Essam family's well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas...

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Dash and Jake set off on a camping trip, determined to follow their wizarding scout rule: stick to the plan and use wits, not wishes. The family soon realise the original plan won’t work, but with teamwork, flexibility and one carefully chosen wish, Rafi helps turn a tricky situation into an exciting, new adventure. She learns that plans don’t have to be perfect, and sometimes changing them leads to something even better.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo learns about fridge magnets and how helpful hooks can be for reaching things like her rubber ducks. Gran Gran decides to teach JoJo about some clever inventions that made life easier. Then JoJo accidentally drops Gran Gran’s keys behind the sofa. It’s time for a Gran Gran plan! They combine the hook from JoJo’s rubber duck game and her hobby horse to make a 'key collector'. But it doesn’t work. JoJo wants to improve her invention, so Gran Gran has another idea – a fridge magnet to attract the keys onto the key collector! Success - the magnet gets the keys, so JoJo and Gran Gran can go to the park. Inventing was fun!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: An excited Sarah and Duck take the Shallots to see the Ribbon Sisters perform with their family circus.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Animation about a family of small creatures who live on, and inside, a planet. Major invents a system so he can hear the other Clangers when they call him for soup.

programme.title: BeddyByes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa discover a dandelion clock and have fun blowing the seeds. At Play Time, they choose another fuzzy flower to play with and bounce on a cloud whilst batting the flower about. The Yummies show MeMo and BaBa how to plant dandelion seeds, and MeMo and BaBa marvel at how a seed can become a beautiful flower. They travel across the Lullaby Lake, making their way to the Bay of Books.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos are playing their music so quickly that everybody does everything very fast. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, and Makka Pakka washes stones until at last, the Tombliboos slow down.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Jade Thirlwall reads a beautiful story about a little boy who dreams of a special outfit.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Blue and Yellow meet a new friend - Green. Discover what happens when you mix blue and yellow.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Baba helps rescue a baby seagull when he gets trapped in fishing twine.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula, Pando and Bing discover a bird's nest while making a playhouse in the shed. They try to find the bird by being super quiet, and she flies into their playhouse. When Bing goes in to look for her, she flies out again; they can't both be in the playhouse at the same time. So Bing decides to give the shed to her until her chicks have all grown up.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster's pineapple costume makes it hard for him to take part in any party events.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Fiona asks the Piggies to help transport her pumpkin to the Vegetable Festival. They agree, but the pumpkin is so big, it's nearly impossible. The Piggies will have to turn it into a carriage!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Pea-Rexes find a stinky flower. The smell transfers to Wasabi, who hates baths.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s Celery performing We’re Here to Grow - if he manages to squeeze himself out of the dressing room on time.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojo and Bo to pull his big balloon to a place where he can snap a picture of a rare bird. But his balloon gets loose, and Mojo and Bo need to get Preston back on the ground.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Wilson has his hands full when an adorable monkey he is babysitting goes bananas.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Carrot is determined to save Supertato from Evil Pea’s trap without help from Prickly Pear

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: 'I couldn't put the lolly back because it was licked, so I said sorry to Padget.' Bing helps Flop do the shopping at Padget's shop, but while Flop is paying, Bing finds a strawberry lolly that he can't resist putting in his pocket. When Flop sees Bing licking the lolly on the way home, they have to decide what to do.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has broken his arm and is struggling to do his usual activities. Petting Arlo hurts, playing with blocks hurts, and even drinking juice hurts. But when Bing and Sula draw together it doesn't hurt, and so he forgets about his arm and does a big, painful 'hoppity voosh'! Amma gently explains to Bing, Sula and Flop that when she broke her arm, she learnt that sometimes you have to ask for help.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is running late to Squirrel Club, so Duggee teaches the squirrels what a commute is – a journey that can take you from home to learn, work or play.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are confused when they see Duggee sitting on a ball because they like to use it differently. Duggee is quick to explain to the Squirrels that there is no right or wrong way to play and that everyone is different. They go on a journey with the ball, watching it bounce, roll and slide from ear to paw as it rebounds between animals. The ball gets hit with a racket, flies through the sky, falls from a cliff and floats in the sea.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: With the help of his hero Ron Allum, Lars plans to dive to the bottom of the Mariana Trench in a submersible called the Deep Sea Challenger. Glitch plans to beat him there by sending a Grimbot in his Grimsub, but it can't withstand the heavy pressure of the water. Lars and Xuli must work together to mount a rescue operation at the bottom of the sea.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Mr Tod crash Jeremy Fisher's annual lakeside get-together, it's up to Peter to save the party.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts follow a strange song to a lonely humpback whale who is alone because he doesn't sound like the other whales!

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag hurts her foot on the job, Vida is there to help fix it. But Zigzag still has to deliver her packages in time for Vida's surprise Vet Day party.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Nori and Preston are creating a circle of standing stones, just like the ancients of Swoppiton used to make. The stones are big - will it take more than Mojo’s crane top to move them?

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: We're turning back the clock with this one. It's the Time Warp Challenge. We time jump through arm circles, runners and toe taps, then head back to the future for the Activate! Get ready to move with Joe and the Activators.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational series using Makaton sign language. Mr Tumble goes on an adventure to the bottom of his garden, while Justin and his friends have an adventurous day out.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey have five minutes to bed, which is just enough time to race to the summit of Mount Mumandad. Facing powerful storms, avalanches and stinky spots will take teamwork.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels become architects so they can design, build and bring to life their very own clubhouse.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels learn the many ways you can walk with the help of friends and a catchy song.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget’s new fitness device sparks a craze on the berg. Running, skating and jumping, the Piris battle it out to score the most points, with a surprise winner.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Dad drops an essential gizmo in a hard-to-reach place. Big Lizard takes them on an underwater adventure to meet Ammon, the tentacled solution to their problem!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger must navigate chaos amid rolling Coconutdons and tail-swatting Pineapplesauruses.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn and Glitch head to the Svalbard Global Seed Vault, hoping to keep some seeds from Glitch's rare fruit tree safe for the future. But, just like when they were cadets together, the two of them get into trouble. Glitch accidentally crashes the Grimbler into the vault, and its hot engines start melting the ice that keeps the seeds cool! With the Go Jetters delayed by a malfunctioning Jet Pad, can Ubercorn and Glitch save the scientists, the vault and Glitch's rare seeds?

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy teams up with the cheating Chipmunk to win a game of basketball.

programme.title: Spidey and His Amazing Friends
programme.desc: Rhino steals a mother goose. Lockjaw stops Black Cat.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: When a flamingo chick gets left behind, it’s up to the Octonauts to save the young bird.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all-weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation - a hi-tech stadium!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: In the perilous plant aisle, Supertato and Evil Pea team up to find treasure as they have half a map each, but she tricks him and steals the map to find the treasure for herself.

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Ben upgrades his supersuit to make himself bolder and more fearless.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. In this doo-wop number, the potatoes dress up in special ways. They get ready for a party and try on silly costumes.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It’s a special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphi has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphi to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Small is pretending to be a detective as something has eaten all the salad in the garden. Wiggly Woo the worm comes to visit, Small flies off on her spoon to find out how rhubarb is harvested, and the two chefs cook a delicious worm crumble for their wiggly guest.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to fly their paper planes. On the way they discover a secret squirrel den, sit on a dragonfly, race like boats and even walk like dinosaurs!

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum is determined to perform Great-Granny Plum’s song at the Balamory ceilidh all by himself, but he soon realises it isn’t easy being a one-man band.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee's nephew Dugley comes to stay. He is one cute puppy, but he looks like he could cause chaos in the clubhouse. And what's that terrible smell? The Squirrels come up with lots of imaginative ways to entertain him but there is only one character that can stop him crying. Who do you think it could be?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Animated deep sea adventure series. Captain Barnacles and his intrepid band of explorers roam the oceans in search of adventure and fun. When hungry gulper eels and undersea tremors threaten Inkling and Kwazii, Dashi uses the new multipurpose Octo Max Suit to rescue them.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit gets a mosquito bite on picture day, and he doesn’t want a photo. Vida has an idea!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Animated adventure comedy series. The Go Jetters visit St Basil's Cathedral, but birthday boy Grandmaster Glitch has glitched it. Not cool!

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: It’s raining, so Rafi and Ruby are playing indoor football. But when they accidentally break a plate, Rafi wishes for it to be fixed but warns them not to play again. When the plate smashes a second time, Rafi wishes for help and summons the clumsy Fixie Pixie, who causes chaos while trying to repair things. Rafi eventually realises it’s better to tell the truth than hide mistakes.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes and Toad see a beautiful sunrise and decide to write a song about it together. But Stripes wants the song to be rocky, while Toad wants it to be slower. They fall out and go off to write their own songs separately. Can Yukee help them become friends again and create one song together?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s Grapefruit dedicating the song All My Friends to the close pals who go with her everywhere.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends are on a pirate adventure – searching for buried treasure! Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is struggling to decide which story to read to Tumble Ted.

programme.title: The Toddler Club
programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Suuvi, Margot, Jasper, Sammie, Teddy and Tehlani, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! The What’s in the Box? game reveals some wooden eggs, a toy chicken and a soft basket, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by things you might find on the farm. This theme is explored through exploration, play, song and story. Grown-ups are encouraged to take part at home by hiding a safe, toddler-appropriate 'on the farm' toy or object inside a recycled box for them to reveal and explore with their toddler! Storytime is all about discovering new words, as Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear visit a farm to feed the animals. They hear lots of different animal noises and try to guess what they are! Viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring the 'on the farm' theme too. Plus, the Benjee and Bailee Say Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with stretching and the Goodbye song.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are upset with each other but can’t remember why.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies set out to give Rebecca a true knight’s adventure, just like in the stories. They build a beautiful castle and take on different roles. It’s the start of an epic quest! But will everything go smoothly with Wilf as the dragon?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join sisters Eden and Ava as they become superheroes! First they create their characters ‘Nature Bunny Girl’ and ‘H-Winter Girl’ – not forgetting new personas for Mum and Dad too! After boosting their superpowers with a super-healthy smoothie, the family of heroes head to the park to try out their skills! It’s not long before they have their first mission: to rescue a toy that’s become lost in the woods. But will the heroes find the toy and save the day?

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all-weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation - a hi-tech stadium!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on the planet of fairy tales, where they learn to play mirror game. They discover that the local alien kids are too tired to play because of their king's never-ending story!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Following clues, Ginger and the Pea-Rexes find Dragonfruitflyus pranking valley animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When a smelly, dirty Waffle runs away at the mention of baths, everyone must find and clean him in time a marching band sing along for their head teacher, Mr Nolan.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Spring is the time of year when lots of different plants grow. Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one that has just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Hank loses a pile of his idea orbs, which causes trouble all around the city.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers learn how butterfly feeders can provide a source of energy for butterflies. They spend time designing their own using overripe fruit to help the butterflies in the garden.

programme.title: Andy's Dino Island
programme.desc: When Jen’s drone goes missing on Dino Island, Andy heads out on an epic adventure to track it down. He discovers one of the island’s inhabitants, a giraffe-sized pterosaur called Hatzegopteryx, is using the drone for a very special purpose. Along the way, he meets a herd of stampeding triceratops.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea has made herself a new and improved Splatterpult, but Supertato turns up, which makes her lose her temper and accidentally fire herself back to her freezer. Evil Pea is about to quit being evil, until her evil parents pay a visit. Mummy and Daddy Pea teach their daughter how to be cool, calm and evil, so she can beat Supertato. After her training is complete, Evil Pea fires snowballs at Supertato and hits him - she’s overjoyed! But when he brings her snowballs back, all three peas lose their tempers.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Click Clack becomes a trainee kite pilot and learns how to navigate a kite as Aimee and Mia perform Mary Poppins’ ‘Let’s Go Fly a Kite’ in British Sign Language, accompanied by animations.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: The caravan is visited by a little owl learning to fly. The owl and Alva, Nick's granddaughter, give Nick the perfect idea for today's song.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Kipp doesn't know what made him giddy, it's up to Vida to solve the dizzy mystery.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A triple solar eclipse is about to happen! Cosima and Big Lizard race down the volcano and hitch a lift on a dragonfly so that they can get vital equipment to Dad in the nick of time.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: At the playground, Khia won’t let Rafi and Ruby have a turn on the zip line. Rafi makes a wish for a magical new zip line that loops through the clouds and zooms around the park. At first, it’s amazing. But Rafi and Ruby ride again and again, forgetting the rule of one turn each. Soon, a long queue forms, and the friends realise they’re doing exactly what Khia did. Rafi decides it’s time to let others have a go. She realises that playground fun works best when everyone gets a turn.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo are playing the quiet game so won't tell Dad which toy to buy for Muffin.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo can’t find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Animation. Sarah and her best friend Duck embark on simple but exciting adventures, exploring the world both near and far in their own wobbly and imaginative way. Scarf Lady takes to the skies in a knitted hot air balloon to race against an old sporting friend.

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola wants to make Charlie's birthday party the best celebration anyone could ever have. The problem is that Lola's idea of a good party isn't anything like Charlie's. Lola has to undo a lot of what she's done but is still really pleased that Charlie's friends love her pink fairy cakes.

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana, Mr Onion, Little Nana and their friends discover a paper star on the ceiling of the little toy house. In Storyland, they find a sky full of twinkly stars to wonder about.

programme.title: BeddyByes
programme.desc: MeMo has made a daisy chain, and BaBa marvels at how the flowers are connected. At the Play Tree, they connect little cars together. Meanwhile, at Yummy Time, they discover their sandwiches are connected by melted cheese. MeMo and BaBa drift on the Lullaby Lake while holding hands, connected. Gramma Leeba explains there are many different connections on the wonderful Planet BeddyByes.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: In a magical forest, colourful characters have adventures and are read bedtime stories. Travelling in the Pinky Ponk, the Tombliboos drink their Pinky Ponk juice. Several times they just avoid spilling their juice by being careful, but finally it splats everywhere.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Gregory Porter sings a special Bedtime Story about how glorious it is to sing in the rain.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s tiny Brussels Sprout performing I’m Mighty and proving how great he is.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: 'If you get shy then your friends can help you.' Bing and his friends put on a show in the park. After Coco's dance, Pando's song and Sula's acrobatics display, everyone is excited to see Bing's finale. When he gets up on the stage, Bing is overcome with stage fright, so his friends and rainbow fairy mice come to the rescue.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy and Laa-Laa drive to the Hidey Hup in the Tubby Car. They meet Tinky Winky and Po and the Teletubbies go on the Tubby Uppy Downy together.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Preschool animation. Tee is super excited when new flat-pack shelving arrives for his room. However, he soon discovers it is going to take some time for Mo to build it.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea plans to freeze Supertato into an ice cube by pretending to teach him to dance.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. Sula tells a frustrated Bing that you have to be loud when you do a show. But Bing is a little too loud, and when Coco joins the show, Bing's loud Biteysaurus isn't welcome, as he is hurting Sula’s ears!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston left an egg sandwich out for too long, and now its explosive smell is stinking up the whole town. Mojo and Bo need to evacuate everyone in Swoppiton before the eggy 'stinkplosion'!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Take a cooking class with Chippo the Cheetah, figure out why different animals are the same, and help the Squirrels stick together and search for Duggee.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie skips into the Clubhouse and greets her friends. Together, they reflect on their first meeting. Arriving with her parents in a long limousine, Norrie, her parents and her 72 siblings flood out of the car. It's just Norrie who is going to stay at the Clubhouse, though, and enjoy some well-needed quiet. She can play alone and hear herself think! But after a while, she misses the hustle and bustle, so Duggee introduces Norrie to Enid the cat. Together, they venture into the Clubhouse, and Norrie has a friend so she isn't alone, but she still has a little bit more room than she would have at home!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey’s bedtime story is disturbed by Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey must find a way to play postman and the ground's lava without squabbling with Bingo.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: When Nine needs a helping hand, he turns into a talented trio of circus blocks, the Three Threes. Learn how to share the number 9 into equal parts with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It's snowing! The Alphablocks whip up a wondrous winter wonderland, but can they get Mr Snowman to the North Pole before he melts? This episode has fun with 'an' and the words man, fun, sun, fan, tan, van and men.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Norman wants to get a pet dinosaur, but Nick and the Popcasters don't think this is a good idea. They try to convince Norman to pick another pet.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: It’s a hot day in Playtime Towers, so Bella helps Lift cool down with her flappy paper fan. This inspires Sketch to make flop-and-fold flutterby butterflies in Boxtropolis. In the Ice Disco, Hettie has a paper fan that’s perfect for the flap dance. Patch has a paper fan too, and it reminds him of the Wowee Peacocks' beautiful tail feathers, the perfect inspiration for his Fruity Fantails!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing finds a frog in the garden and wants to keep it, so he and Sula make the frog a 'house' in a bucket. But Froggy doesn't like her new home and hops out. Maybe they've not thought enough about what Froggy really needs in her home.

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘cucumber’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see cucumbers that are crunchy, mushy in the middle and curly. Dee meets a cool cucumber, and we meet a very cute baby one. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Declan causes a stir when he decides not to say 'please'.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Dana wants to cheer Wilf up and asks the Piggies to find something that will make him happy. But Wilf takes advantage of their kindness and requests a honking vehicle - just to annoy everyone! When it breaks, Wilf is even more miserable.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a kitchen, where they watch a man turn on a tap and fill a glass with water. When he leaves, the Twirlywoos come out and turn the tap on. They fill a glass with water and then start to find other things around the kitchen to fill up, progressing to sillier and sillier objects - ending with a wellington boot! Just as the sink fills up and begins to overflow, the Hooter calls the Twirlywoos home, and the man comes back to find his kitchen full of odd containers filled with water. Back in the Big Red Boat, it's time for tea. The fruit on top of the tea machine is a giant watermelon, which is so full of tea that the Twirlywoos have to go back for second and third helpings. They are so full that they have to lie down, leaving the coast clear for Peekaboo to come and drink up all the leftover tea.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends bake some delicious pancakes, whilst Mr Tumble and Baker Tumble make some of their own to feed to the other hungry Tumbles!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po drive the Tubby Car to the Hidey Hup and take turns on the Tubby Bouncy. Tinky Winky and Dipsy arrive and they have a go at bouncing too.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Remi, George, Zadie, Idris, Felicity, Tommy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyone’s bags in this episode are cardboard boxes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate cardboard boxes inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song ‘The Wheels on the Bus!’ features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Plus, the Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a building site with her new friend Dreya to play a game of I-spy.

programme.title: What’s in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Gardening expert Sue introduces the Fact Pack to roses. They discover that there are hundreds of types, why roses can smell lovely, and how their smell and flavour is in many different things.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It’s a special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphi has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphi to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: The gang rescue a trapped baby Watermelonsuchus with the help of some Lycheemodus.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While away camping, Bluey makes a new friend called Jean-Luc. Despite speaking different languages, they come up with a great game. Bluey is sad when Jean-Luc goes home unexpectedly but learns that even if friendships are fleeting they can still mean a lot.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasn't loud at all but impossibly quiet! As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in. But Roly is still too shy, so Duggee and Norrie start doing things with him, knowing this will help him to embrace the fun of the Clubhouse. While painting, playing with balloons and yelling, Roly begins to truly find his voice and be himself! Soon, Roly hardly noticed if his dad was around at all.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver returns with a song all about seasons. It’s a celebration of spring, summer, autumn and winter.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to the park to feed the birds at the pond. On the way, they race like racing cars, spot the different coloured flowers, listen to the thunder and even meet a Stick Man tree!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sisters Kyra and Khloe go on a big family day out to a farm. There are lots of exciting things to do, including a pony ride, or should that be a unicorn ride? They explore an amazing maze where the girls find a giant sunflower! But will they ever find their way back out? They also discover a very muddy and squelchy pumpkin patch and pick their perfect pumpkins! This Bafta-winning pre-school observational documentary follows the lives of young children and their families from a variety of backgrounds across the UK.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself. Dill pickle is a popular Polish ingredient and Kay gets his friends to taste it before they eat the soup. Not all of his friends like the taste at first, but Kay hopes they will all enjoy the finished soup. As well as dill pickle, he uses spring onions, potatoes and carrot in his soup. He then serves the delicious, hot soup in baked bread rolls with whipping cream on top.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: She goes on a training rescue mission with a lifeboat team to find out how the equipment on a lifeboat works. She shows us how the special thermal-imaging cameras are used to find people who are in trouble - even at night! Next, she visits a quarry to see how gravel, sand, cement and water are mixed together to make concrete. Maddie uses her special cameras to follow the journey the ingredients take from the quarry to the plant where everything is mixed together. We see how the mixing lorry then takes the concrete to the building site and how it is poured to make a road!

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks is a disability assistance dog for her owner Charli, who has a condition that makes it painful for her to pick things up or to push and pull things. Tinks uses her super fetch skills to help Charli every day. Charli is helping park ranger Pauline with some litter picking in Woofton Park. First they have to travel across the tricky terrain in the park to meet Pauline, so she can give them the instructions and equipment they need. Tinks persuades Charli to use the motor on Charli’s wheelchair at turbo speed! Tinks helps Charli find the litter, but the gate to the recycling bins is locked, and the recycling van is on its way. Can Tinks fetch park ranger Pauline in time to recycle their rubbish?

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some of the zoo’s acrobats, a family of fossa who are incredible climbers and enjoy jumping high up in the trees. Next, they go to see the leaf-tailed gecko, which has an amazing tail that looks just like a leaf to help camouflage it in the wild. Steve and Aneeshwar then reveal the surprising wild connection between them.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is unwell. He's in his pyjamas and under strict orders to stay in bed and rest. But poor Waffle can't get comfy. When he sees Evie and Simon playing in the garden, he feels left out and begins to woof and woof. Simon tells Waffle to rest quietly, but it's too late. The wonder dog's incessant barking has already disturbed Mrs Hobbs next door and, to make matters worse, like Waffle she's not feeling well either. Mrs Hobbs has a bad cold, and she's blaming it on the Brooklyn-Bells. She's convinced getting drenched in their ball-pit pond is the cause. The Brooklyn-Bells do chores for Mrs Hobbs to help her get better, and Jess brings home a surprise from work.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba help the seagulls defeat the Great Gull.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: It's the day the geese fly over on their annual migration south. When Sonny reports that geese put on an amazing display if you mimic their calls, the gang look for the right sound to get a response from the geese before they fly past!

programme.title: Maddie + Triggs
programme.desc: Mum is taking Maddie and Triggs to McGinty’s farm to hear his world-famous animal orchestra. But McGinty’s star performer, Gary the Chicken, won’t come out of his coop! Can Maddie use her musical skills and expert diplomacy to save the day?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: After breaking the Chickens' TV aerial during the finale of their favourite soap opera, the Squirrels take it upon themselves to recreate the final episode for them. But will their performance impress the grumpy Chickens?

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks are on an adventure exploring Fraction Island. They are curious to learn why there are four islands when their map only shows one. When they meet a new friend, One Whole, they realise that they need to explore all the islands and find more Fraction Friends if they want to solve the mystery!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock H has built a henhouse and needs a HEN to live in it. She and her friends end up making three hens and decide to give them names. The pen shows them capital letters, and Alphablock C explains that they need them for names. They name their hens PEN, JEN and ZEN. The hens love their name badges. Then, C introduces a parade of animals with different names, each one wearing a badge - CAM the ram, PAT the cat and ZAK the yak. There’s just one more name to be given - BEN the pen!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends learn all about getting their eyes tested. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble with some new glasses when he loses his old pair.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, then forwards again!

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how grasses play such an important role in the environment. They find out that grasses can be food for animals and some can be turned into ingredients to make food for people too. On the way, they discover how long grass can help shelter small animals and insects, and that cut grass can be useful too, before finding a lovely grassy place to relax and have fun!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running. Her aim is to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies' very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move because of propellers! Grace flies a seaplane, shows off an unusual hydrocopter and goes for a fast ride in a raceboat!

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: An unexpected visitor has arrived in the playground - a cat! The pups are initially scared. After all, they’ve heard that cats have lasers for eyes. But Dodge decides to be brave and talk to it. Dodge discovers the cat is friendly and isn’t anything like how the pups had imagined, she’s just lost. The pups decide to help her find her way to Kitten School.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is finding it hard to think of something new to do on Do Something New Day, so he heads to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny’s house to see if she has any ideas.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Kwazii and Tracker visit the desert for an Octo-Agent test but end up getting lost.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter and his friends mistake the echoey 'rebbits' of a lost Jeremy Fisher for a scary monster, they set about ridding their tunnels of this mysterious invader. But with Tommy Brock on the warpath nearby, it won't be easy!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels try the ultimate achievement of counting to 100 to earn their counting badge.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzag’s ADHD gives her a case of the hops, so Vida teaches her how to make a to-do list.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: It's movie night, and Supertato is thrilled to discover that the star is Prickly Pear - his special skill is rounding up Mini Peas with his pea-yodelling. But the film gives Evil Pea the idea to take over Tato Tower with Mini Peas, trapping Supertato inside. When Supertato is accidentally ejected out of Tato Tower, he must convince Prickly Pear to take up pea-yodelling again so they can both wrangle the Mini Peas back to the freezer.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings are throwing a party in the attic. Weasy thinks it’s a great idea until he realises that grown-ups are not invited! Feeling left out, he decides to throw his own, with chaotic results!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo are great at picking apples with Mojo’s crane top. But Preston’s drone might be even better... until it accidentally scoops up Felix. Mojo and Bo are on the chase!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey’s bedtime story is disturbed by Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Exhausted with requests for help, Looph deliberately disappears. The Piris turn detective, searching the berg with little success until her ingenious plan is finally revealed.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey must find a way to play postman and the ground's lava without squabbling with Bingo.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum jumps to conclusions when he hears about an upcoming volcanic eruption. But little does he know, it's Ava’s science project that’s set to fizzle in a safe experiment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Preschool comedy drama series. Jess hopes the Essam family's well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas...

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Dash and Jake set off on a camping trip, determined to follow their wizarding scout rule: stick to the plan and use wits, not wishes. The family soon realise the original plan won’t work, but with teamwork, flexibility and one carefully chosen wish, Rafi helps turn a tricky situation into an exciting, new adventure. She learns that plans don’t have to be perfect, and sometimes changing them leads to something even better.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo learns about fridge magnets and how helpful hooks can be for reaching things like her rubber ducks. Gran Gran decides to teach JoJo about some clever inventions that made life easier. Then JoJo accidentally drops Gran Gran’s keys behind the sofa. It’s time for a Gran Gran plan! They combine the hook from JoJo’s rubber duck game and her hobby horse to make a 'key collector'. But it doesn’t work. JoJo wants to improve her invention, so Gran Gran has another idea – a fridge magnet to attract the keys onto the key collector! Success - the magnet gets the keys, so JoJo and Gran Gran can go to the park. Inventing was fun!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: An excited Sarah and Duck take the Shallots to see the Ribbon Sisters perform with their family circus.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Animation about a family of small creatures who live on, and inside, a planet. Major invents a system so he can hear the other Clangers when they call him for soup.

programme.title: BeddyByes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa discover a dandelion clock and have fun blowing the seeds. At Play Time, they choose another fuzzy flower to play with and bounce on a cloud whilst batting the flower about. The Yummies show MeMo and BaBa how to plant dandelion seeds, and MeMo and BaBa marvel at how a seed can become a beautiful flower. They travel across the Lullaby Lake, making their way to the Bay of Books.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos are playing their music so quickly that everybody does everything very fast. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, and Makka Pakka washes stones until at last, the Tombliboos slow down.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Jade Thirlwall reads a beautiful story about a little boy who dreams of a special outfit.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Blue and Yellow meet a new friend - Green. Discover what happens when you mix blue and yellow.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Baba helps rescue a baby seagull when he gets trapped in fishing twine.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula, Pando and Bing discover a bird's nest while making a playhouse in the shed. They try to find the bird by being super quiet, and she flies into their playhouse. When Bing goes in to look for her, she flies out again; they can't both be in the playhouse at the same time. So Bing decides to give the shed to her until her chicks have all grown up.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster's pineapple costume makes it hard for him to take part in any party events.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Fiona asks the Piggies to help transport her pumpkin to the Vegetable Festival. They agree, but the pumpkin is so big, it's nearly impossible. The Piggies will have to turn it into a carriage!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Pea-Rexes find a stinky flower. The smell transfers to Wasabi, who hates baths.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s Celery performing We’re Here to Grow - if he manages to squeeze himself out of the dressing room on time.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojo and Bo to pull his big balloon to a place where he can snap a picture of a rare bird. But his balloon gets loose, and Mojo and Bo need to get Preston back on the ground.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Wilson has his hands full when an adorable monkey he is babysitting goes bananas.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Carrot is determined to save Supertato from Evil Pea’s trap without help from Prickly Pear

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: 'I couldn't put the lolly back because it was licked, so I said sorry to Padget.' Bing helps Flop do the shopping at Padget's shop, but while Flop is paying, Bing finds a strawberry lolly that he can't resist putting in his pocket. When Flop sees Bing licking the lolly on the way home, they have to decide what to do.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has broken his arm and is struggling to do his usual activities. Petting Arlo hurts, playing with blocks hurts, and even drinking juice hurts. But when Bing and Sula draw together it doesn't hurt, and so he forgets about his arm and does a big, painful 'hoppity voosh'! Amma gently explains to Bing, Sula and Flop that when she broke her arm, she learnt that sometimes you have to ask for help.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is running late to Squirrel Club, so Duggee teaches the squirrels what a commute is – a journey that can take you from home to learn, work or play.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are confused when they see Duggee sitting on a ball because they like to use it differently. Duggee is quick to explain to the Squirrels that there is no right or wrong way to play and that everyone is different. They go on a journey with the ball, watching it bounce, roll and slide from ear to paw as it rebounds between animals. The ball gets hit with a racket, flies through the sky, falls from a cliff and floats in the sea.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: With the help of his hero Ron Allum, Lars plans to dive to the bottom of the Mariana Trench in a submersible called the Deep Sea Challenger.
