Glitch plans to beat him there by sending a Grimbot in his Grimsub, but it can't withstand the heavy pressure of the water. Lars and Xuli must work together to mount a rescue operation at the bottom of the sea.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Mr Tod crash Jeremy Fisher's annual lakeside get-together, it's up to Peter to save the party.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts follow a strange song to a lonely humpback whale who is alone because he doesn't sound like the other whales!

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag hurts her foot on the job, Vida is there to help fix it. But Zigzag still has to deliver her packages in time for Vida's surprise Vet Day party.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Nori and Preston are creating a circle of standing stones, just like the ancients of Swoppiton used to make. The stones are big - will it take more than Mojo’s crane top to move them?

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: We're turning back the clock with this one. It's the Time Warp Challenge. We time jump through arm circles, runners and toe taps, then head back to the future for the Activate! Get ready to move with Joe and the Activators.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational series using Makaton sign language. Mr Tumble goes on an adventure to the bottom of his garden, while Justin and his friends have an adventurous day out.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey have five minutes to bed, which is just enough time to race to the summit of Mount Mumandad. Facing powerful storms, avalanches and stinky spots will take teamwork.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels become architects so they can design, build and bring to life their very own clubhouse.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels learn the many ways you can walk with the help of friends and a catchy song.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget’s new fitness device sparks a craze on the berg. Running, skating and jumping, the Piris battle it out to score the most points, with a surprise winner.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Dad drops an essential gizmo in a hard-to-reach place. Big Lizard takes them on an underwater adventure to meet Ammon, the tentacled solution to their problem!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger must navigate chaos amid rolling Coconutdons and tail-swatting Pineapplesauruses.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn and Glitch head to the Svalbard Global Seed Vault, hoping to keep some seeds from Glitch's rare fruit tree safe for the future. But, just like when they were cadets together, the two of them get into trouble. Glitch accidentally crashes the Grimbler into the vault, and its hot engines start melting the ice that keeps the seeds cool! With the Go Jetters delayed by a malfunctioning Jet Pad, can Ubercorn and Glitch save the scientists, the vault and Glitch's rare seeds?

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy teams up with the cheating Chipmunk to win a game of basketball.

programme.title: Spidey and His Amazing Friends
programme.desc: Rhino steals a mother goose. Lockjaw stops Black Cat.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: When a flamingo chick gets left behind, it’s up to the Octonauts to save the young bird.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all-weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation - a hi-tech stadium!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: In the perilous plant aisle, Supertato and Evil Pea team up to find treasure as they have half a map each, but she tricks him and steals the map to find the treasure for herself.

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Ben upgrades his supersuit to make himself bolder and more fearless.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. In this doo-wop number, the potatoes dress up in special ways. They get ready for a party and try on silly costumes.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It’s a special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphi has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphi to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Small is pretending to be a detective as something has eaten all the salad in the garden. Wiggly Woo the worm comes to visit, Small flies off on her spoon to find out how rhubarb is harvested, and the two chefs cook a delicious worm crumble for their wiggly guest.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to fly their paper planes. On the way they discover a secret squirrel den, sit on a dragonfly, race like boats and even walk like dinosaurs!

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum is determined to perform Great-Granny Plum’s song at the Balamory ceilidh all by himself, but he soon realises it isn’t easy being a one-man band.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee's nephew Dugley comes to stay. He is one cute puppy, but he looks like he could cause chaos in the clubhouse. And what's that terrible smell? The Squirrels come up with lots of imaginative ways to entertain him but there is only one character that can stop him crying. Who do you think it could be?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Animated deep sea adventure series. Captain Barnacles and his intrepid band of explorers roam the oceans in search of adventure and fun. When hungry gulper eels and undersea tremors threaten Inkling and Kwazii, Dashi uses the new multipurpose Octo Max Suit to rescue them.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit gets a mosquito bite on picture day, and he doesn’t want a photo. Vida has an idea!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Animated adventure comedy series. The Go Jetters visit St Basil's Cathedral, but birthday boy Grandmaster Glitch has glitched it. Not cool!

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: It’s raining, so Rafi and Ruby are playing indoor football. But when they accidentally break a plate, Rafi wishes for it to be fixed but warns them not to play again. When the plate smashes a second time, Rafi wishes for help and summons the clumsy Fixie Pixie, who causes chaos while trying to repair things. Rafi eventually realises it’s better to tell the truth than hide mistakes.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes and Toad see a beautiful sunrise and decide to write a song about it together. But Stripes wants the song to be rocky, while Toad wants it to be slower. They fall out and go off to write their own songs separately. Can Yukee help them become friends again and create one song together?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it’s Grapefruit dedicating the song All My Friends to the close pals who go with her everywhere.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends are on a pirate adventure – searching for buried treasure! Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is struggling to decide which story to read to Tumble Ted.

programme.title: The Toddler Club
programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Suuvi, Margot, Jasper, Sammie, Teddy and Tehlani, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! The What’s in the Box? game reveals some wooden eggs, a toy chicken and a soft basket, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by things you might find on the farm. This theme is explored through exploration, play, song and story. Grown-ups are encouraged to take part at home by hiding a safe, toddler-appropriate 'on the farm' toy or object inside a recycled box for them to reveal and explore with their toddler! Storytime is all about discovering new words, as Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear visit a farm to feed the animals. They hear lots of different animal noises and try to guess what they are! Viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring the 'on the farm' theme too. Plus, the Benjee and Bailee Say Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with stretching and the Goodbye song.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are upset with each other but can’t remember why.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies set out to give Rebecca a true knight’s adventure, just like in the stories. They build a beautiful castle and take on different roles. It’s the start of an epic quest! But will everything go smoothly with Wilf as the dragon?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join sisters Eden and Ava as they become superheroes! First they create their characters ‘Nature Bunny Girl’ and ‘H-Winter Girl’ – not forgetting new personas for Mum and Dad too! After boosting their superpowers with a super-healthy smoothie, the family of heroes head to the park to try out their skills! It’s not long before they have their first mission: to rescue a toy that’s become lost in the woods. But will the heroes find the toy and save the day?

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all-weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation - a hi-tech stadium!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on the planet of fairy tales, where they learn to play mirror game. They discover that the local alien kids are too tired to play because of their king's never-ending story!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Following clues, Ginger and the Pea-Rexes find Dragonfruitflyus pranking valley animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When a smelly, dirty Waffle runs away at the mention of baths, everyone must find and clean him in time a marching band sing along for their head teacher, Mr Nolan.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Spring is the time of year when lots of different plants grow. Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one that has just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Hank loses a pile of his idea orbs, which causes trouble all around the city.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers learn how butterfly feeders can provide a source of energy for butterflies. They spend time designing their own using overripe fruit to help the butterflies in the garden.

programme.title: Andy's Dino Island
programme.desc: When Jen’s drone goes missing on Dino Island, Andy heads out on an epic adventure to track it down. He discovers one of the island’s inhabitants, a giraffe-sized pterosaur called Hatzegopteryx, is using the drone for a very special purpose. Along the way, he meets a herd of stampeding triceratops.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea has made herself a new and improved Splatterpult, but Supertato turns up, which makes her lose her temper and accidentally fire herself back to her freezer. Evil Pea is about to quit being evil, until her evil parents pay a visit. Mummy and Daddy Pea teach their daughter how to be cool, calm and evil, so she can beat Supertato. After her training is complete, Evil Pea fires snowballs at Supertato and hits him - she’s overjoyed! But when he brings her snowballs back, all three peas lose their tempers.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Click Clack becomes a trainee kite pilot and learns how to navigate a kite as Aimee and Mia perform Mary Poppins’ ‘Let’s Go Fly a Kite’ in British Sign Language, accompanied by animations.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: The caravan is visited by a little owl learning to fly. The owl and Alva, Nick's granddaughter, give Nick the perfect idea for today's song.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Kipp doesn't know what made him giddy, it's up to Vida to solve the dizzy mystery.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A triple solar eclipse is about to happen! Cosima and Big Lizard race down the volcano and hitch a lift on a dragonfly so that they can get vital equipment to Dad in the nick of time.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: At the playground, Khia won’t let Rafi and Ruby have a turn on the zip line. Rafi makes a wish for a magical new zip line that loops through the clouds and zooms around the park. At first, it’s amazing. But Rafi and Ruby ride again and again, forgetting the rule of one turn each. Soon, a long queue forms, and the friends realise they’re doing exactly what Khia did. Rafi decides it’s time to let others have a go. She realises that playground fun works best when everyone gets a turn.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo are playing the quiet game so won't tell Dad which toy to buy for Muffin.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo can’t find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Animation. Sarah and her best friend Duck embark on simple but exciting adventures, exploring the world both near and far in their own wobbly and imaginative way. Scarf Lady takes to the skies in a knitted hot air balloon to race against an old sporting friend.

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola wants to make Charlie's birthday party the best celebration anyone could ever have. The problem is that Lola's idea of a good party isn't anything like Charlie's. Lola has to undo a lot of what she's done but is still really pleased that Charlie's friends love her pink fairy cakes.

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana, Mr Onion, Little Nana and their friends discover a paper star on the ceiling of the little toy house. In Storyland, they find a sky full of twinkly stars to wonder about.

programme.title: BeddyByes
programme.desc: MeMo has made a daisy chain, and BaBa marvels at how the flowers are connected. At the Play Tree, they connect little cars together. Meanwhile, at Yummy Time, they discover their sandwiches are connected by melted cheese. MeMo and BaBa drift on the Lullaby Lake while holding hands, connected. Gramma Leeba explains there are many different connections on the wonderful Planet BeddyByes.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: In a magical forest, colourful characters have adventures and are read bedtime stories. Travelling in the Pinky Ponk, the Tombliboos drink their Pinky Ponk juice. Several times they just avoid spilling their juice by being careful, but finally it splats everywhere.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Gregory Porter sings a special Bedtime Story about how glorious it is to sing in the rain.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.desc: The Burger Kitchen (Part 1/2): In the first half of a two-part Nightmare, Gordon tries to save a burger joint in LA, run by a dysfunctional family in a state of constant conflict. (S5 Ep3) [S]

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The Burger Kitchen (Part 2/2): In the second half of a two-part Nightmare in LA, can Gordon Ramsay bring together the fragmented Saffron family and save their failing restaurant? (S5 Ep4) [S]

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Rob Brydon and Joe Wilkinson. With Huge Davies in Dictionary Corner. (S28 Ep5) [S]

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Bewilderness
programme.desc: Bill's imagination works overtime at Swansea's Grand Theatre as he mixes genius musical parodies with illogical absurdity and bizarre stories from his colourful career. [S]

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: It's July 12th and the Quinn family want to get out of Derry before the Orange Parades start. Aunt Sarah's tarot cards predict a disaster for their trip - and she might be right. (S1 Ep5/6) [AD,S]

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin's over the moon when she becomes editor of the school magazine, Orla's obsessed with step aerobics, Da Gerry is in trouble with Ma Mary, and there's romance for Aunt Sarah. (S1 Ep6/6) [AD,S]

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Christmas Special
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr oversees the disputes on the festive topics that set the great British public's chins wagging. With Sean Lock, Jon Richardson, Sarah Millican, Stephen Mangan and Joe Wilkinson. [S]

programme.desc: (2022) Jamila Gray stars as Lil' Law- a struggling young rapper who wants to make her fortune to help her family in director Sanaa Lathan's hip-hop-battling-coming-of-fame drama. [S]

programme.title: Broken Arrow (1950)
programme.desc: (1950) Western starring James Stewart. A gold prospector who's tired of reprisal killings offers a truce to an Apache leader. But tensions resurface.

programme.title: Siege of the Saxons
programme.desc: (1963) Dark ages adventure. King Arthur is murdered and usurped by his champion knight (Ronald Howard), so an outlaw (Ronald Lewis) seeks the mystic Merlin (John Laurie) to right the wrong. [S]

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: (1963) Epic, iconic historical drama with Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton. To protect her realm, the storied Egyptian queen runs rings around the smitten Mark Anthony and beguiled Julius Caesar.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: (2019) Luc Besson's action thriller about a young Russian assassin, with Sasha Luss. A scout offers Anna an opportunity to become a model in Paris, but can she escape her links to the KGB?

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: (2019) Partially animated action film based on a popular manga comic. Alita, a deadly cyborg brought back to life by a mourning scientist, sets out to discover her true identity. [AD,S]

programme.title: Sheroes
programme.desc: (2023) She-heroes: action-with-attitude. Three young women transform into a Generation Z version of Charlie's Angels to rescue their mate from a drug lord in Thailand. Cue guns and bikinis. [S]

programme.desc: (1946) Sherlock Holmes murder-mystery starring Basil Rathbone and Nigel Bruce. Holmes and Dr Watson are employed to protect a priceless diamond. But it's snatched from under their noses.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: (2014) Jamie Foxx stars in this reboot of the 1982 musical. Orphan Annie is transported to post-2008 recession-hit New York, where she becomes the ward of a germaphobe running for mayor.

programme.title: Rosaline
programme.desc: (2022) Literary romcom with Kaitlyn Dever and Isabela Merced. This is the story of Juliet's cousin who gets dumped by Romeo at the start of Shakespeare's play... and she's not too pleased.

programme.title: Man V. Food
programme.desc: Lake Tahoe provides a stunning backdrop as Casey Webb explores the area's best eats. He finds a complete dinner in a bun and a Greek comfort food staple. S6/Ep7

programme.title: Man V. Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Las Vegas, NV. He gambles on grilled cheese, tackles a towering sushi creation and faces the Kasespatzle Challenge at a German beer hall. S6/Ep8

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy is excited to create an old-school wedding cake - tall, ornate and filled with live birds! A dad-to-be orders a lobster tail pastry for his wife. S1/Ep7

programme.desc: The team visit a museum for a prehistoric mammal cake. A Sweet 16 cake proves complicated and Frankie makes and decorates a cake all by himself. S1/Ep8

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes beef skewers, veg and blue cheese mayo for a quick summertime dinner. Plus, she whips up Pesto chicken and blueberry, lemon and ricotta bruschetta. S19/Ep5

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. Ina gets cheesy with parmesan. She serves up orecchiette with broccoli rabe and sausage, parmesan chive mashed potatoes, and parmesan and chipotle popcorn. S14/Ep1

programme.title: The Great British Bake-Off
programme.desc: The ten bakers have much to prove with a fruity teatime Signature, an international-flavoured Technical, and an ambitious Showstopper. S9/Ep3

programme.title: The Great British Bake-Off
programme.desc: The bakers must create a cracking meringue signature bake. Plus, they have a wobbly technical challenge, and a complex chocolate Showstopper. S9/Ep4

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James and chef Martin Blunos explore the food on offer in the picturesque town of Vannes. They use local ingredients to make a special monkfish meal. S1/Ep4

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James Martin demonstrates the classic technique for preparing and cooking scallops. Plus, he is joined by chef Martin Blunos to cook cod in beer batter. S1/Ep5

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James Martin and chef Martin Blunos explore the charming port of Pornic. They cook local scallops with shrimp butter and make a special strawberry tart. S1/Ep6

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James Martin continues his food adventure in the beautiful port city of Saint-Malo. He is joined by chef Lawrence Keogh to cook some delicious veal. S1/Ep7

programme.title: Rick Stein's Fruits Of...
programme.desc: ...The Sea. Rick makes delicious dishes using local, Cornish delicacies. He catches some fine mullet and for the first time attempts to make a fish tagine using a Moroccan recipe. S1/Ep2

programme.title: Rick Stein's Fruits Of The Sea
programme.desc: Rick travels to France to recall some of his early culinary influences. He samples great sea food dishes in Bayonne and famous for the best seafood in France, Marseilles. S1/Ep3

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares her portable food ideas. Breakfast bagel burgers are the perfect way to start the day and taco chicken Caesar salad wraps are ideal on-the-go bites. S19/Ep4

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes beef skewers, veg and blue cheese mayo for a quick summertime dinner. Plus, she whips up Pesto chicken and blueberry, lemon and ricotta bruschetta. S19/Ep5

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: In the popular North African holiday destination, Yotam dines on fresh fish, explores the national obsession with fiery harissa paste, and samples spicy merguez sausages. S1/Ep3

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Turin tasting the most iconic foods named after this city including an amazing breaded beef cutlet and a veal and tuna favourite. S1/Ep5

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Genoa tasting the most iconic foods named after the city, including a pasta dish made with pesto Genovese and a fish stew called burrida. S1/Ep6

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is exploring America finding the best joints that combine home cooking with home brewing. First, he's in Chicago and then in Tampa Bay, Florida. S12/Ep7

programme.title: Man V. Food
programme.desc: In Sacramento, Adam visits the California State Fair for fried food on a stick. Later, he tucks into five pounds of chili-smothered hot dogs in the Knucklehead Challenge! S2/Ep37

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a soul food joint in Washington serving saucy chicken wings. Plus, he samples a bacon-wrapped and deep-fried hot dog in Kansas City. S10/Ep5

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy is looking for big flavour. In Tennessee he visits a place serving lamb with Syrian spiced tomato sauce. In Minnesota a café serves wild rice French toast. S10/Ep6

programme.title: Man V. Food
programme.desc: In Tampa, Casey Webb devours the ultimate Cuban platter. He stops at a speakeasy with a secret and accepts the 20-minute Cookie Munchers Challenge. S6/Ep9

programme.title: Man V. Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Orlando, FL. He faces a famous footlong and takes part in a team challenge with hometown hero, boyband superstar Joey Fatone. S6/Ep10

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team are like kids in a toy store - literally, when they are asked to make a toy shop-themed cake. They also have a beach-themed cake to create. S1/Ep9

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team has to make a traditional Chinese dragon cake and are busy preparing cannoli shells for the annual $25 Throw Back Cannoli Day. S1/Ep10

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri travels throughout the country looking for real-deal bar food. In a small bar in Philadelphia, he eats a vegan club sandwich with deep fried tofu. S11/Ep1

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is checking out the unexpected. In Sacramento, he visits a former Japanese grill turned diner that serves European classics. S11/Ep5

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri heads to Boulder to taste a butter rum caramel cake. In Rego Park, New Jersey, a Jewish deli cooks up stuffed cabbage and kreplach. S11/Ep9

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is specialising in pork. He tries a chilli with smoked, braised pork. Plus, some fans show Guy their favourite burrito place with pork green chilli sauce. S11/Ep13

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. The pressure is on as Ina Garten makes a breakfast buffet for her foodie friends! Dishes include potato pancetta frittata and overnight Belgian waffles. S12/Ep7

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. Ina Garten's photographer friend is coming for the weekend. She treats him to a menu that includes zucchini and goats cheese tart and tomato carpaccio. S12/Ep8

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. It's all about chicken as Ina shares her kitchen tips for roasted chicken with radishes. And, a twist on a classic with chicken and spinach Waldorf salad. S13/Ep1

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. Ina covers some easy but tasty midweek suppers. She makes roasted salmon tacos with avocado salsa, then cider roasted pork tenderloin. S13/Ep2

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. Ina tackles daunting dishes with easy techniques, like a perfectly risen soufflé. Guest star Jose Andres has an amazing trick for separating eggs. S13/Ep4

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back To...
programme.desc: ...Basics. Ina does a master class in how to cook eggs, from soft boiled to using them in a dressing. Chef Jose Andres reveals the secret of his perfectly fried eggs. S13/Ep5

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking For...
programme.desc: ...Less. With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1/Ep6

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shows how fresh fish can make a cheap dish. He cooks a roast side of salmon with feta and new potatoes, turning the leftovers into a tasty salmon filo pie. S1/Ep3

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Chef Jamie Oliver cooks a roast chicken dinner and shows how the leftovers can make delicious budget meals including chickens stew with dumplings. S1/Ep4

programme.title: James Martin's: Island To...
programme.desc: ...Highlands. James's Scottish adventure starts in Edinburgh aboard the luxurious Royal Scotsman. Top chef Nick Nairn joins him to eat in its stunning dining room. S1/Ep17

programme.title: James Martin's: Island To...
programme.desc: ...Highlands. James heads to the stunning Cairngorms National Park. He's joined by chef pal Nick Nairn for a leg that includes whitewater rafting on the River Spey. S1/Ep18

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The duo are back in Scotland, in beautiful Dumfries and Galloway. It's a place close to their hearts, a rural paradise with a glorious coastline. S1/Ep2

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The lads explore Dave's home county of Lancashire. They tour a crisp-making potato farm and create a dish celebrating the local spuds. S1/Ep3

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: For their next adventure in the west of Britain, Dave and Si explore Merseyside, an area renowned for its musical heritage and cultural identity. S1/Ep4

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers continue their journey in northwest Wales, discovering a host of inspiring local producers celebrating the area's rich diversity. S1/Ep5

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri tries old favourites and new specialities from around America. In Detroit, he enjoys Hungarian pancakes with goulash. And, in Vancouver he eats a modern dish. S12/Ep13

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is on the look-out for one-of-a-kind meals. He visits a Southern/Jewish fusion restaurant that serves Bubby's brisket with beans and rice. S11/Ep4

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is crossing the country for comfort food. In Pineville, he finds a tavern in an 18th century farmhouse that serves snapping turtle soup. S11/Ep8

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy is traveling the country looking for comfort food. In New Orleans, he discovers a place from the 1890s where they serve jambalaya and green bean casserole. S11/Ep12

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: In Massachusetts, Guy visits an Italian eatery cooking fresh New England lobster ravioli. In Minnesota a diner serves burger patties stuffed with a range of fillings. S10/Ep7

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia where they deep-fry handmade empanadas. Plus, he checks out a waterfront lobster eatery in Portland, Maine. S10/Ep8

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins, And Dives
programme.desc: Guy follows the culinary directions given by viewers. In Kentucky, he visits a 1951 drive-in that is still serving the same burger that made it famous. S10/Ep4

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: This show sees the return of some of the most memorable campmates from I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here! They survived the Australian jungle or the Welsh castle, but South Africa is an even harder proposition which will push them to their limits and test them like never before. In the penultimate episode of the series, the remaining contestants take on Cyclone. Will they be victorious and win their dinner for this evening or will it be rice and beans. The celebs remaining at the end of this episode will move into the live final, where one of them will be crowned a legend.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. But don't let the ducklings and the quaint cafe fool you - behind the smiles and the sandwiches, the knives are already out. The households live side by side 24/7 and try to keep their neighbours' onside to avoid being voted out during 'a removal'. They have a chance to save themselves from the removals by winning immunity in the neighbourhood challenges. Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: The first episode of a series that follows chef Jason Atherton in Dubai as he showcases some fantastic local ingredients, celebrated foodie hotspots, and brilliant local chefs as he cooks up some delicious recipes that are inspired by the region. Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town and shows us how to spice up our life Dubai-style - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: The first episode of a series that follows chef Jason Atherton in Dubai as he showcases some fantastic local ingredients, celebrated foodie hotspots, and brilliant local chefs as he cooks up some delicious recipes that are inspired by the region. Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town and shows us how to spice up our life Dubai-style - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to picturesque Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region - inspired by a visit to a distillery, James uses cognac for his first alfresco cook-up before following in the footsteps of the Queen Mother and visiting the Chateau Venteuil, the home of the De La Rochefoucauld family. James meets a miller who still uses traditional methods to make award-winning brioche in the shadow of the chateau - he then creates a superb salad which incorporates some of the region's best produce and includes the brilliant brioche. Back in the UK, James makes a classic French onion soup and pays homage to the patron saint of pastry chefs with a stunning dessert.

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Get ready for a brand-new and hilarious reality game show that's jam-packed with all your favourite celebrities! Each week Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry are set secret missions to sabotage the unsuspecting contestants of totally fake TV shows, all to build up a secret prize pot! Along the way they'll be joined by special celebrity guests from Emma Willis to Clare Balding. This final episode of the series sees the celebs take on 'The Backstabber' - a haunting reality show hosted by Rylan Clark, who is totally in on the whole thing. But that's not all as actress Fay Ripley joins Mission HQ to get involved with all the mayhem as this week's rebellious Guest Saboteur. Expect chaos, carnage and calamity!

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. The removal of the first household sends ripples through the Neighbourhood. One family is left reeling by the vote they received and their sights are set on revenge. New arrivals take centre stage as they attempt to integrate into the close. Distinct bonds and divisions begin to emerge as everyone finds their footing in the game. Overnight, the close is invaded by Gnomes for the second neighbourhood immunity challenge - which exposes some cutthroat gameplay. One family comes to blows over plans to use dirty tactics, threatening to fracture their relationship. With the next removal just around the corner, whose time on the close is nearly up?

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. But don't let the ducklings and the quaint cafe fool you - behind the smiles and the sandwiches, the knives are already out. The households live side by side 24/7 and try to keep their neighbours' onside to avoid being voted out during 'a removal'. They have a chance to save themselves from the removals by winning immunity in the neighbourhood challenges. Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: The first episode of a series that follows chef Jason Atherton in Dubai as he showcases some fantastic local ingredients, celebrated foodie hotspots, and brilliant local chefs as he cooks up some delicious recipes that are inspired by the region. Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town and shows us how to spice up our life Dubai-style - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to picturesque Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region - inspired by a visit to a distillery, James uses cognac for his first alfresco cook-up before following in the footsteps of the Queen Mother and visiting the Chateau Venteuil, the home of the De La Rochefoucauld family. James meets a miller who still uses traditional methods to make award-winning brioche in the shadow of the chateau - he then creates a superb salad which incorporates some of the region's best produce and includes the brilliant brioche. Back in the UK, James makes a classic French onion soup and pays homage to the patron saint of pastry chefs with a stunning dessert.

programme.title: Jurassic Park: The Lost World
programme.desc: Jeff Goldblum and Julianne Moore, 1997. A research team's observation project on the newly-released dinosaurs of Jurassic Park is threatened.

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Get ready for a brand-new and hilarious reality game show that's jam-packed with all your favourite celebrities! Each week Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry are set secret missions to sabotage the unsuspecting contestants of totally fake TV shows, all to build up a secret prize pot! Along the way they'll be joined by special celebrity guests from Emma Willis to Clare Balding. This final episode of the series sees the celebs take on 'The Backstabber' - a haunting reality show hosted by Rylan Clark, who is totally in on the whole thing. But that's not all as actress Fay Ripley joins Mission HQ to get involved with all the mayhem as this week's rebellious Guest Saboteur. Expect chaos, carnage and calamity!

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: The first episode of a series that follows chef Jason Atherton in Dubai as he showcases some fantastic local ingredients, celebrated foodie hotspots, and brilliant local chefs as he cooks up some delicious recipes that are inspired by the region. Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town and shows us how to spice up our life Dubai-style - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to picturesque Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region - inspired by a visit to a distillery, James uses cognac for his first alfresco cook-up before following in the footsteps of the Queen Mother and visiting the Chateau Venteuil, the home of the De La Rochefoucauld family. James meets a miller who still uses traditional methods to make award-winning brioche in the shadow of the chateau - he then creates a superb salad which incorporates some of the region's best produce and includes the brilliant brioche. Back in the UK, James makes a classic French onion soup and pays homage to the patron saint of pastry chefs with a stunning dessert.

programme.title: Jurassic Park: The Lost World
programme.desc: Jeff Goldblum and Julianne Moore, 1997. A research team's observation project on the newly-released dinosaurs of Jurassic Park is threatened.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley take on the challenge and hope to win thousands of pounds for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Get ready for a brand-new and hilarious reality game show that's jam-packed with all your favourite celebrities! Each week Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry are set secret missions to sabotage the unsuspecting contestants of totally fake TV shows, all to build up a secret prize pot! Along the way they'll be joined by special celebrity guests from Emma Willis to Clare Balding. This final episode of the series sees the celebs take on 'The Backstabber' - a haunting reality show hosted by Rylan Clark, who is totally in on the whole thing. But that's not all as actress Fay Ripley joins Mission HQ to get involved with all the mayhem as this week's rebellious Guest Saboteur. Expect chaos, carnage and calamity!

programme.title: New: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Britain's Got Talent goes live on Saturday nights for the Semi-Finals as the acts who impressed the judges during auditions must now compete for a place in the grand finale. [S] S19 Ep9

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. The removal of the first household sends ripples through the Neighbourhood. One family is left reeling by the vote they received and their sights are set on revenge. New arrivals take centre stage as they attempt to integrate into the close. Distinct bonds and divisions begin to emerge as everyone finds their footing in the game. Overnight, the close is invaded by Gnomes for the second neighbourhood immunity challenge - which exposes some cutthroat gameplay. One family comes to blows over plans to use dirty tactics, threatening to fracture their relationship. With the next removal just around the corner, whose time on the close is nearly up?

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: National and international news with reports and analysis from ITV's Correspondents in the UK and around the world. [S]

programme.title: Minority Report
programme.desc: Intriguing crime action based loosely on noted author Philip K Dick's 1956 novella The Minority Report. In the year 2054, crime is virtually eliminated from Washington, D.C. thanks to an elite law-enforcing squad that uses gifted humans with special powers to see into the future and predict crimes beforehand - John Anderton heads the team and believes steadfastly in the system's flawlessness... that is, until the day the squad predicts that Anderton will commit a murder in the next 36 hours.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: This show sees the return of some of the most memorable campmates from I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here! They survived the Australian jungle or the Welsh castle, but South Africa is an even harder proposition which will push them to their limits and test them like never before. In the penultimate episode of the series, the remaining contestants take on Cyclone. Will they be victorious and win their dinner for this evening or will it be rice and beans. The celebs remaining at the end of this episode will move into the live final, where one of them will be crowned a legend.

programme.title: Minority Report
programme.desc: Intriguing crime action based loosely on noted author Philip K Dick's 1956 novella The Minority Report. In the year 2054, crime is virtually eliminated from Washington, D.C. thanks to an elite law-enforcing squad that uses gifted humans with special powers to see into the future and predict crimes beforehand - John Anderton heads the team and believes steadfastly in the system's flawlessness... that is, until the day the squad predicts that Anderton will commit a murder in the next 36 hours.

programme.title: English Football League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from across the Football League, featuring West Brom v Ipswich, Norwich v Swansea, Sheffield United v Preston and Stoke v Portsmouth. [S,HD]

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: This show sees the return of some of the most memorable campmates from I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here! They survived the Australian jungle or the Welsh castle, but South Africa is an even harder proposition which will push them to their limits and test them like never before. In the penultimate episode of the series, the remaining contestants take on Cyclone. Will they be victorious and win their dinner for this evening or will it be rice and beans. The celebs remaining at the end of this episode will move into the live final, where one of them will be crowned a legend.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. But don't let the ducklings and the quaint cafe fool you - behind the smiles and the sandwiches, the knives are already out. The households live side by side 24/7 and try to keep their neighbours' onside to avoid being voted out during 'a removal'. They have a chance to save themselves from the removals by winning immunity in the neighbourhood challenges. Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?
